theme: Light mode primary for clinical clarity; Dark mode supported.

visual_enhancers.surfaces: Neumorphism (Clay & Crisp variant) - Flat Solid surfaces, no heavy glassmorphism to prevent visual refraction of clinical data.
visual_enhancers.borders: Use sharp 1px borders (border-black/5 or border-border) to ground elements.
visual_enhancers.radius: Max 8px border-radius (rounded-lg) for a sharp but safe clinical feel.

layout_mapping.main_workspace: col-span-12 lg:col-span-9 grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6

layouts.spacing: Generous internal padding (p-6, p-8) within cards.
layouts.alignment: Left-align forms and text. Center align empty states.
layouts.headers: Solid background headers with hard bottom border. Never transparent.

rules: Micro-interactions only. No heavy 3D or parallax.
Hover states: Slight lift (-translate-y-0.5) and shadow-md for interactive cards.
Copy-to-clipboard: Quick pulse animation and color flash to green on success.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure APCA standards or WCAG AA 4.5:1 for all text. No gray-on-gray.
accessibility.focus: focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/50 focus:outline-none on all inputs and buttons.
accessibility.data_testid: MANDATORY on all buttons, inputs, form fields, and copy actions (e.g., data-testid='copy-short-narrative').

image_urls.description: Bright modern dental clinic. Use as a subtle background for the login/auth page or an empty state, with a heavy white/gray overlay (bg-white/80).

3d_libraries.recommendation: None. This is a high-density, text-heavy clinical tool. 3D elements would introduce unnecessary cognitive load and distraction.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install required dependencies: yarn add @phosphor-icons/react sonner
Use .js/.jsx extensions for all React components. Do not use .tsx.
Override default tailwind colors to match the Organic & Earthy palette.
Implement the bento layout for the main dashboard (Inputs on Left, Outputs on Right).
Do NOT use HTML <select> or <input type='date'>. Use Shadcn components.
Make sure the 'Copy to Clipboard' functionality is robust and provides a Sonner toast feedback.
Avoid the 'AI purple gradient' entirely. The AI generation should feel like a reliable clinical tool, not magic.
Implement a Radiograph requirement advisor using visual badges (Shadcn Alert or Badge) indicating PA, BW, Pano, or CBCT needs based on the procedure.
Include data-testid on every interactive element.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like robot, brain, lightbulb for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, custom selection states, and loading animations separate good from extraordinary.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute.
GENERAL UI UX DESIGN GUIDELINES: Reduce Cognitive Load: Surface only essential data; avoid cluttered forms or nested dropdowns.
Calm Visual Language: Avoid harsh reds or flashing alerts; use soft greens/blues and calm animations to build trust.
Information Hierarchy: Prioritize the generated narratives and actions (copying) by allocating adequate screen real estate.
F-Pattern Scanning: Ensure the most critical workflow actions follow natural left-to-right, top-to-bottom reading patterns.
